perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five years beginning on the date hereof; provided that the obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement shall survive the expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be for a period of five years beginning on the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'provided that the obligations relating to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement shall survive the expiration of this Agreement', we create uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the intent of the original clause and introduces ambiguity regarding the long-term protection of confidential information. Clause 6 is now contradictory to this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the disclosing party and the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party for the disclosing party. No interest, license or any right respecting the Confidential Information, other than set out herein, is granted to the receiving party hereunder by implication or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that confidentiality obligations survive the agreement's expiration. The changed text removes this provision, creating uncertainty about the duration of these obligations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Confidential Information shall be held in trust and confidence by the receiving party. Without the survival clause, it's unclear how long this trust and confidence should last, potentially conflicting with the disclosing party's rights to the confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Confidential Information shall not be copied, reproduced in any form or stored in a retrieval system or data base by the receiving party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except for such copies and storage as may reasonably be required internally by the receiving party in connection with the Opportunity', we're creating a situation where all internal copying or storage is prohibited unless consented to, which is a huge change from before. This now means the paragraph contradicts paragraph 4 in regards to employees who require the storage of this information to perform duties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its employees, directors, officers, advisors, attorneys, auditors and agents only on a need-to-know basis in connection with the Opportunity, and each employee, director, officer, advisor, attorney, auditor or agent to whom Confidential Information is disclosed shall treat such Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement. The receiving party shall be liable for all acts and omissions of each person and entity to which the receiving party discloses Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for internal copies and storage of confidential information as reasonably required. The changed text removes this exception, creating a blanket prohibition on copying or storing confidential information without prior written consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows disclosure to employees on a need-to-know basis. If employees need to store or copy the information as part of their duties, the changed text now prohibits this without consent, even if it's on a need-to-know basis as outlined in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict and a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as defined.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party agrees and acknowledges that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies are not the exclusive remedies for a breach by the receiving party, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party agrees and acknowledges that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement by the receiving party or any of its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives or professional advisors and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies are the exclusive remedies for a breach by the receiving party, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Such remedies are not the exclusive remedies' to 'Such remedies are the exclusive remedies' directly contradicts the remainder of the clause. The original language allowed for all available remedies in law/equity. The modification limits it only to equitable relief (injunction, specific performance). This now contradicts clause 11, limiting its scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the disclosing party and its shareholders, directors, officers, employees, representatives and professional advisors from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including reasonable legal fees and the cost of enforcing this Agreement) owed to a third party arising out of the breach by the receiving party of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the remedies available to the disclosing party in the event of a breach by the receiving party. Originally, the disclosing party had access to all remedies available at law or in equity. The changed text limits these remedies to equitable relief (injunction, specific performance). This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the receiving party to 'indemnify, defend and hold harmless' the disclosing party from any damage, loss, cost or liability, including legal fees. This indemnity clause provides a remedy (monetary compensation) that is no longer available under the changed text's 'exclusive remedies' provision. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists, satisfying the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written. This Agreement is neither intended to nor shall it be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership or other form of business association between the parties, or any obligation of the parties to enter into any agreement relating to the Opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, express and implied, oral and written.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'This Agreement is neither intended to nor shall it be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership or other form of business association between the parties, or any obligation of the parties to enter into any agreement relating to the Opportunity.' now allows the agreement to be interpreted as being the creation of some type of business relationship between the parties. This now contradicts clause 13 which states that there is no obligation between the parties until a definitive agreement has been entered. The original clause was to clarify that there was no immediate obligation until a separate agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a transaction between the parties has been executed and delivered, neither of the parties will be under any obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. Each party further acknowledges and agrees that the other party reserves the right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals with regard to a transaction between the parties and to terminate discussions and negotiations at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement should not be construed as creating a joint venture, partnership, or other form of business association, and that there was no obligation to enter into any further agreement. The changed text removes this explicit statement. This removal contradicts the clause stating that neither party is under any obligation until a final definitive agreement is executed. By removing the clarifying language, the agreement could be interpreted as creating some form of obligation prior to a definitive agreement, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text.
